description: Creates new links on structure nodes in the range. If the node already has a link associated with it, this will be ignored. Existing links can be removed with the structure link delete command. If source and target ID numbers are not supplied, then the command will automatically look for valid targets on all potential new links in the range.

6.0.description: Creates new links on structure nodes in the range. If the node already has a link associated with it, this will be ignored. Existing links can be removed with the structure link delete command. If source and target ID numbers are not supplied, then the command will automatically look for valid targets on all potential new links in the range.

keywords.name: default-attach
keywords.description: By default a new link created with this command is given rigid connections in all six degrees-of-freedom if created on side on, and free connection in all six if created on side two. By setting this keyword to true, links created will be given the default attachment conditions associated with the first element their host node is connected to. This is not compatible with target node. Please note that this is required for elements to pass property values to the link models in individual degrees-of-freedom. If not done, this will not happen even if the link models match those expected by the element.

7.0.description: Creates new links on structure nodes in the range. If the node already has a link associated with it, this will be ignored. Existing links can be removed with the structure link delete command. If source and target ID numbers are not supplied, then the command will automatically look for valid targets on all potential new links in the range.

keywords.name: default-attach
keywords.description: By default a new link created with this command is given rigid connections in all six degrees-of-freedom if created on side on, and free connection in all six if created on side two. By setting this keyword to true, links created will be given the default attachment conditions associated with the first element their host node is connected to. This is not compatible with target node. Please note that this is required for elements to pass property values to the link models in individual degrees-of-freedom. If not done, this will not happen even if the link models match those expected by the element.

keywords.name: on-nodeid
keywords.description: Specify a specific node component-id number on which to create a link. If this keyword is used, the range phrase is not used. If the target keyword is used with a specific id supplied, on-nodeid must be used to specify the source node.

keywords.name: offset
keywords.description: When a specific target has not been specified, an offset vector can be used to search for a node or zone that does not lie in the location of the source node. The location checked will be the source node position plus the offset vector v. Note that if a zone is the target and an offset is used, then the large strain sliding update should be turned off using the sliding property of the element.

keywords.name: side
keywords.description: Specify on which side (side number can be 1 to N) of a node attached to an embedded liner element the link should attach to. The default is side 1.

keywords.name: target
keywords.description: Identify the target entity. The default target zone will be used if target is not supplied.

keywords.name: target-zone
keywords.description: Specify the link(s) should look for a zone target. If i is not specified, then a non-null zone that is within a distance \(\delta\) of the source node is utilized. If i is specified, then that zone is used if it is non-null and the source node is within a distance \(\delta\) of its boundary. (See the preceding discussion for a definition of \(\delta\).)

keywords.name: target-node
keywords.description: Specify that the link(s) should look for a node target. If i is not specified, then a node that is within a distance \(\delta\) of the source node is utilized. If i is specified, then the two nodes must be near to each other for a link to be created. Near is defined as being within a distance \(\delta\) of one another, where \(\delta\) is the global value of node tolerance (see the structure link tolerance-contact command). The attachment conditions of the new link are set to “rigid” for all six directions if the link is on side 1, and “free” if the link is on side 2.

keywords.name: target-range
keywords.description: Specify a range that will be used to filter target objects (specified by the target keyword). Only objects that fall within the range will be considered for link creation. By default all objects of that type will be considered.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Assign the created link to the group named s1. If the optional slot s2 is supplied, the group is assigned to the slot named s2; if omitted, the group is assigned to the slot named Default. Use of the group logic is described in Groups.

keywords.name: default-attach
keywords.description: By default a new link created with this command is given rigid connections in all six degrees-of-freedom if created on side on, and free connection in all six if created on side two. By setting this keyword to true , links created will be given the default attachment conditions associated with the first element their host node is connected to. This is not compatible with target node . Please note that this is required for elements to pass property values to the link models in individual degrees-of-freedom. If not done, this will not happen even if the link models match those expected by the element.

keywords.name: on-nodeid
keywords.description: Specify a specific node component-id number on which to create a link. If this keyword is used, the range phrase is not used. If the target keyword is used with a specific id supplied, on-nodeid must be used to specify the source node.

keywords.name: offset
keywords.description: When a specific target has not been specified, an offset vector can be used to search for a node or zone that does not lie in the location of the source node. The location checked will be the source node position plus the offset vector v . Note that if a zone is the target and an offset is used, then the large strain sliding update should be turned off using the sliding property of the element.

keywords.name: side
keywords.description: Specify on which side (side number can be 1 to N) of a node attached to an embedded liner element the link should attach to. The default is side 1 .

keywords.name: target-node
keywords.description: Identify the target entity. The default target zone will be used if target is not supplied. Specify the link(s) should look for a zone target. If i is not specified, then a non-null zone that is within a distance \(\delta\) of the source node is utilized. If i is specified, then that zone is used if it is non-null and the source node is within a distance \(\delta\) of its boundary. (See the preceding discussion for a definition of \(\delta\) .) Specify that the link(s) should look for a node target. If i is not specified, then a node that is within a distance \(\delta\) of the source node is utilized. If i is specified, then the two nodes must be near to each other for a link to be created. Near is defined as being within a distance \(\delta\) of one another, where \(\delta\) is the global value of node tolerance (see the structure link tolerance-contact command). The attachment conditions of the new link are set to “rigid” for all six directions if the link is on side 1 , and “free” if the link is on side 2 .

keywords.name: target-range
keywords.description: Specify a range that will be used to filter target objects (specified by the target keyword). Only objects that fall within the range will be considered for link creation. By default all objects of that type will be considered. See the Range Phrase documentation for the list of range elements and control keywords that may be included in a range phrase. Note the keyword range is not used in this context.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Assign the created link to the group named s1 . If the optional slot s2 is supplied, the group is assigned to the slot named s2 ; if omitted, the group is assigned to the slot named Default . Use of the group logic is described in Groups .
